questions.question: Which Indian state is the first to set up a Gene Bank Project?
questions.explanation: Maharashtra has announced to set up a ‘Gene bank project’, to conserve native and endangered animals, crops, marine and biological species. It will be implemented by the Maharashtra State Biodiversity Board (MSBB) and Rs 172.39 crore has been allocated for the next five years. The project will work on seven themes — marine, crop, veterinary, freshwater, grassland biodiversity, protection and management of forest right areas, and regeneration of forests. The project will focus on mitigating the impact on the food chain due to climate change.

questions.question: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship signed a MoU with which Indian organisation to upskill its technical staff?
questions.explanation: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) signed a MoU (Memorandum of Understanding) with the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO), to up-skill the technical staff. More than 4000 ISRO technical employees will be trained in the programme over the course of the next 5 years. It aims to create a formal framework for short-term courses for capacity- building of the technical staff of ISRO as per the industry requirements.

questions.question: ‘World of Advance Virtual Experience (WAVE)’ is an initiative of which Indian public sector bank?
questions.explanation: Indian Bank launched the digital transformation initiative – World of Advance Virtual Experience ‘WAVE’. The bank has recently launched its first digital product “Pre-Approved Personal Loan (PAPL)” that aims to provide customers with instant loan disbursements, serviced through digital channels only. PAPL loan product can be availed through mobile application, website and internet banking facility.

questions.question: Which institution releases the ‘Report on Currency and Finance (RCF)’?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) released the ‘Report on Currency and Finance (RCF)’ for the year 2021-22. As per the report, price stability is a necessary precondition for strong and sustainable growth. It also proposed a seven-point blueprint for reforms. The Indian economy may take 12 years to overcome the losses emanating from the COVID-19 pandemic, which account to Rs 52 Lakh Crores.

questions.question: The Madras High Court has invoked the “parens patriae jurisdiction” to declare which entity as a ‘living being’?
questions.explanation: The Madras High Court has invoked the “parens patriae jurisdiction” to declare Mother Nature as a Living Being with all corresponding rights, duties and liabilities of a living person. The order was spelt while responding to the petition from an official who sought to quash the proceedings against him for allegedly granting land deed for government land classified as ‘Forest Land’. The court said indiscriminate destruction of nature will endanger the existence of flora and fauna.

questions.question: India’s first green-field grain-based ethanol plant has been inaugurated in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar inaugurated the country’s first greenfield grain-based ethanol plant in Purnea. The 105-crore plant by Eastern India Biofuels is the first one developed since the Centre gave approval to Bihar’s ethanol production and promotion policy-2021. The state government has proposed opening at least 17 ethanol plants over the next two years.

questions.question: Project-75 (India) or P-75I is associated with which Indian Armed Force?
questions.explanation: P-75 India (P-75I) is the second project to build submarines for the Indian Navy, after first phase of P-75, signed in 2005 with France. The P-75I phase seeks the construction of six conventional submarines with better sensors and weapons and the Air Independent Propulsion System (AIP). France’s Naval Group recently exited from the project as the French Navy does not use AIP, which was required by the Indian Navy. The announcement came a day before Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s trip to France.

questions.question: Harshada Sharad Garud, who was seen in the news, is associated with which sports?
questions.explanation: Harshada Sharad Garud made history by becoming the first Indian to win a gold medal in Weightlifting, at the IWF Junior World Championships at Heraklion in Greece. She lifted 153-kilogram in the 45-kilogram weight category, to finish on top of the podium. Before the 18-year old sportsperson, there are only two Indians to win a medal in IWF Junior World Championships. Mirabai Chanu had won a bronze in 2013 and Achinta Sheuli won a silver medal last year.

questions.question: Which Indian state has launched a comprehensive healthcare programme for newly-wed tribal couples?
questions.explanation: Attappadi, Kerala’s only tribal block has recorded increasing infant deaths, mainly due to malnutrition and health problems of mothers. Since 2012, as many as 137 tribal infant deaths have occurred. The Health Department of the state launched a comprehensive healthcare programme for all newly-wed tribal couples in Attappadi. It aims to improve the health of tribal women and prevent neonatal deaths and children born with genetic disorders.

questions.question: Which states have the highest ratio of undertrials in jails, as per latest NCRB Data?
questions.explanation: According to the latest data compiled by the National Crime Records Bureau (NCRB) for 2020, of the total 4,88 lakh prison inmates, 76 % or 3.71 lakh were found to be undertrials, of which about 68% were either illiterate or school dropouts. Among the undertrials, about 20% were Muslims, while about 73% were Dalits, tribals or OBCs. Delhi and Jammu and Kashmir (J&K) were found to have the highest ratio of undertrials in jails at 91%, followed by Bihar and Punjab at 85%, and Odisha at 83%.
